articles.title: DELIVERY
articles.summary: Atlassian surveyed engineering teams to see who's actually getting value from AI. Their research [4] found that fragmented tools, context switching, and information silos are still the biggest productivity killers for engineering teams — and that AI alone doesn't fix them.

articles.title: Secure your spot for practical strategies to quantify AI ROI
SOFTWARE FACTORIES AND THE AGENTIC MOMENT
articles.summary: StrongDM has developed a "Software Factory" where AI agents autonomously write and refine code based on specifications and scenarios, removing human involvement in coding and review. One of the main innovations making this possible is its "Digital Twin Universe," a system of behavioral clones of third-party services that allows for high-volume, safe, and cost-effective validation of software scenarios.

articles.title: HOW WE MADE GEO JOINS 400× FASTER WITH H3 INDEXES
articles.summary: Geo joins use spatial predicates and are computationally expensive because they lack a clean join key, leading to quadratic complexity. To overcome this, the Floe database automatically rewrites these queries to use Uber's H3 indexes. H3 indexes partition geographical areas into hierarchical hexagonal cells, converting complex spatial intersections into efficient equi-joins on compact integer keys.

articles.title: WE MOURN OUR CRAFT
articles.summary: AI is diminishing the programmer's craft, consuming human work, and reducing their role to mere oversight. Despite this, there is a career necessity for professionals to embrace these tools to remain competitive.

articles.title: STOP GENERATING, START THINKING
articles.summary: A seasoned engineer is uneasy about the industry's rapid adoption of LLM-generated code. He's worried about outsourcing critical thinking, as LLMs are best at generation but not as great at reasoning like a human is.

articles.title: TWO KINDS OF AI USERS ARE EMERGING. THE GAP BETWEEN THEM IS ASTONISHING
articles.summary: Martin sees two kinds of AI users emerging: power users who've embraced tools like Claude Code and are flying, and everyone else stuck on ChatGPT or Microsoft's enterprise Copilot — which is so bad that Microsoft itself uses Claude Code internally. The result is small teams with API access and coding agents massively outproducing enterprises trapped behind locked-down IT and legacy software.

articles.title: TRACK USER RETENTION AUTOMATICALLY WITH CLERK
articles.summary: You're tracking signups but probably not what happens after. Clerk's new retention report measures how users come back after sign-up: 30-day, 8-week, and 3-month cohorts with industry benchmarks. No analytics setup required.

articles.title: GITHUB AGENTIC WORKFLOWS
articles.summary: GitHub Agentic Workflows enable repository automation by running AI coding agents within GitHub Actions. Users define these continuous AI workflows in natural language using Markdown files, which are then compiled into GitHub Actions to automate tasks like code improvements, issue triage, documentation, and analysis. They integrate deeply with GitHub, support multiple AI engines like Copilot, Claude, and Codex, and prioritize safety with sandboxed execution and minimal permissions.

articles.title: MATCHLOCK
articles.summary: Matchlock is a CLI tool designed to run AI agents within secure, ephemeral microVMs, preventing unrestricted access to your machine. It achieves this by providing network allowlisting, injecting secrets in-flight (so they never enter the VM), and blocking all other connections by default. Agents operate in a full, isolated Linux environment that boots quickly and is disposable, ensuring that even malicious actions don't compromise your credentials or data.

articles.title: WHY E CORES MAKE APPLE SILICON FAST
articles.summary: Apple silicon Macs achieve their high performance mainly due to their Efficiency (E) cores, which handle numerous background tasks, rather than solely relying on Performance (P) cores. Immediately after startup, E cores are heavily used for processes like Spotlight indexing and initial backups, leaving the P cores largely free for user applications. This architecture is managed by macOS's Quality of Service (QoS) system, which prioritizes foreground tasks for P cores and restricts background tasks to E cores, even when P cores are idle.

articles.title: THE CLOUD AGENT THESIS
articles.summary: Cloud agents like Devin shift AI coding from local pair programming to organizational delegation. Running on remote infrastructure, these tools allow non-engineers to trigger tasks via Slack or Jira. This model scales capacity asynchronously, moving the primary bottleneck from code generation to automated review.

articles.title: DEBUGGING WITH AI: CAN IT REPLACE AN EXPERIENCED DEVELOPER?
articles.summary: This dev tested Claude on three real React/Next.js bugs ranging from easy to hard. It aced the simple one, half-fixed the medium one (but introduced new issues), and completely failed the tricky one, confidently going in circles with wrong fixes. AI is a great first pass for standard bugs, but knowing when to stop prompting and start thinking is the real skill.

articles.title: AGENT SLACK
articles.summary: Agent Slack is a CLI specifically designed to enable AI agents to automate various interactions within Slack.

articles.title: (AI) SLOP TERRIFIES ME
articles.summary: AI's ability to produce merely "good enough" software (or "slop") might create widespread user apathy, ultimately leading to the demise of quality software craftsmanship.

articles.title: A CASE OF THE REVERSE IVORY TOWER
articles.summary: Platform engineering creates a reverse ivory tower when rigid abstractions distance infrastructure from developer reality.
